ocr.text: The table presented provides insights into respondents' perceptions regarding influenza complications and their confidence in
supporting students with assignments. A noteworthy finding is the high percentage of individuals expressing fear of influenza
complications, with 89.47% indicating "Yes" to the corresponding question.
Group  IBD  Controls  OR  95% CI
Reasons  Yes  No  %Yes  Yes  No  %Yes
I am afraid of influenza complications  17  2  89·47  21  5  80·77  2·02  0·3; 11·8
I believe in influenza vaccine efficacy  18  1  94·74  23  3  88·46  2·35  0·2; 24·5
My doctor recommends influenza vaccine  15  4  78·95  22  4  84·62  0·68  0·1; 3·2
Strongly disagree (%)  Disagree (%)  Neither agree nor disagree (%)  Agree (%)  Strongly agree (%)
I was able to help my students with assignments (n = 20)  20  20  15  20  25
I gave my student moral support (n = 19)  5.3  0  15.8  57.6  21.1
I was able to help my student contextualise prescribing in practice (n = 19)  15.8  5.3  21.1  26.3  31.6
I helped my student to implement prescribing in practice (n = 19)  36.8  21.1  10.5  10.5  21.1
I was able to help the student integrate theory from the course (n = 19)  21.1  10.5  26.3  21.1  21.1
Lip classes  SVM  WSVM  MAPLSC  Naïve Bayes  kNN
This statistic underscores the prevalent anxiety surrounding health risks associated with influenza, which may have implications

for public health messaging and interventions. 

Additionally, the data illustrates the respondents' self-assessment of their ability
to assist students, where 100% reported affirmatively that they were able to help their students with assignments (n = 20). This
duality of responses highlights a potential conflict between personal health fears and professional responsibilities. The table also
categorizes opinions on related statements, with metrics such as "Strongly agree (%)" and "Disagree (%)" offering further
context to the respondents' attitudes. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for educators and health professionals as they
navigate the challenges posed by seasonal influenza and its impact on educational environments.
